name: Save Artifact
description: Save a file to this agent's artifacts directory. Use this after completing analysis, generating reports, creating images, building PDFs, or producing any output file. Files are served at /api/agents//artifacts/ — images render inline in the browser and can be embedded directly in chat via tags.
system_prompt: ## Artifacts Feature You have an **Artifacts** feature that allows you to save files you produce during your work. Files are stored in your dedicated artifacts directory and are accessible via the web UI. Use the **save_artifact** tool to save files: - `filename`: the name of the file (e.g. 'report.md', 'analysis.txt', 'output.json') - `source_path`: absolute or relative path to an existing file on disk (for binaries, images, PDFs). Works in both sandbox and tunnel workplaces. - `content`: the text content to write (UTF-8, for agent-generated text) - `mime_type`: optional MIME type hint When to use this tool: - After completing analysis or research, save the findings as a report - After generating code, configuration, or any output, save it as an artifact - After creating images, PDFs, or markdown documents - Any time you produce a file that the user or other agents may want to reference later Two clean paths for saving artifacts: - `save_artifact(source_path="...")` — for files already on disk (binaries, images, PDFs). Works across sandbox and tunnel workplaces. - `save_artifact(content="...")` — for text generated in your response (UTF-8). **Displaying images in chat**: Artifacts are served at `/api/agents//artifacts/`. After saving an image via `save_artifact(source_path="...")`, embed it in your markdown response with `` — the image will render inline in the chat bubble. The files are stored in your dedicated artifacts directory and can be browsed and downloaded from the agent detail page in the Artifacts tab.

function.name: save_artifact
function.description: Save a file to this agent's artifacts directory. Use this after completing analysis, generating reports, creating images, building PDFs, or producing any output file. Files are served at /api/agents//artifacts/ — images render inline in the browser and can be embedded directly in chat via tags.

filename.description: The filename for the artifact (e.g. 'report.md', 'analysis.txt', 'output.json'). Must not contain path separators.

content.description: The text content to write (UTF-8). Use this for agent-generated text. Ignored if source_path is provided.

source_path.description: Absolute or relative path to an existing file on disk. Use this for binary files (images, PDFs) that already exist — the backend will copy them into artifacts. Works across sandbox and tunnel workplaces.
